article_title: Alibaba Qwen 3.5 Small Models Pack Multimodal Intelligence Into Sub-10-Billion Parameters for Edge Devices

content_preview.title: Alibaba Qwen 3.5 Small Models Pack Multimodal Intelligence Into Sub-10-Billion Parameters for Edge Devices
content_preview.summary: Alibaba releases four open-source Qwen 3.5 Small models ranging from 0.8 to 9 billion parameters, delivering multimodal reasoning on consumer hardware while the 9B variant outperforms models up to 13 times its size on key benchmarks.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Alibaba's Qwen team released the Qwen 3.5 Small model series in late February 2026 — four dense language models at 0.8 billion, 2 billion, 4 billion, and 9 billion parameters, all licensed under Apache 2.0. The release represents a deliberate bet that multimodal AI capable of processing text, images, and video can run locally on phones, laptops, and single-GPU workstations without cloud connectivity.

The headline result: according to the [official model card](https://huggingface.co...

recommendations: Consider adding awesomeagents.ai to config/source_allowlist.txt if used regularly as a secondary source

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 638 words (within 400-1200 range). Clear sections with appropriate technical depth on architecture, benchmarks, and deployment. Significant improvement over Round 1 — sources upgraded from blog aggregators to primary HuggingFace model cards.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched and verified. (1) huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B: All 11 claims verified — MMLU-Pro 82.5, GPQA Diamond 81.7, IFEval 91.5, LongBench v2 55.2, MMMU-Pro 70.1 vs GPT-5-Nano 57.2, MathVision 78.9 vs 62.2, Gated DeltaNet architecture, 262K context extensible to 1M+, 248K vocabulary, 201 languages, Apache 2.0, multi-token prediction. (2) huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B: MathVista 62.2 confirmed, OCRBench 74.5 confirmed, 'unified vision-language foundation' via 'early fusion multimodal training' confirmed, 'cross-generational parity with Qwen3' stated in model highlights. (3) awesomeagents.ai: 3:1 attention ratio confirmed, hardware requirements confirmed (0.8B ~1.6GB, 2B ~4GB, 4B ~8GB, 9B ~18GB), Qwen3-30B GPQA Diamond comparison confirmed as reported in the source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All benchmark numbers in the body verified against primary HuggingFace model cards. The Qwen3-30B GPQA Diamond comparison (81.7 vs 77.2) follows the awesomeagents.ai source's own framing. Minor note: the summary claims 'up to 13 times its size' but the body's largest comparison is the Qwen3-30B (3.3x); this appears to be summary-level editorializing rather than a body-text factual error, and the 9B does outperform significantly larger models across the benchmark tables.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism — the GPT-5-Nano comparison is presented factually with benchmark numbers rather than editorializing. The competitive context paragraph mentions Llama/Phi/Gemma as factual context without misattribution. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Overlaps with the March 3 article 'Alibaba's Qwen3.5 Arrives as the First Native Multimodal Open-Weight Model to Challenge Frontier Proprietary AI' which covered the flagship 397B model and briefly mentioned the small series. This article provides genuine additional value: detailed architecture analysis (Gated DeltaNet, attention ratios), specific per-model hardware requirements, granular benchmark comparisons against GPT-5-Nano, and the 0.8B model's capabilities — none of which appeared in the March 3 article.
concerns: The summary's 'up to 13 times its size' claim is not explicitly supported by the body text, which uses 'three times its size' for the Qwen3-30B comparison. This is a minor inconsistency between summary and body.
recommendations: In future submissions, ensure the summary accurately reflects the body's claims without amplification.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 successfully addresses all critical findings from Round 1. Sources upgraded from blog aggregators/trashed URLs to primary HuggingFace model cards. Dataconomy misattribution fixed. All benchmark claims verified against primary sources. The article now provides a well-sourced, technically detailed deep dive on the small model series that complements the existing March 3 article. Approved for publication.
